title: cassia javanica subsp. agnes
intro: Cassia javanica subsp. agnes, also known as Agnes' Cassia, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. Agnes' Cassia is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, making it a valuable herb for overall well-being. Cassia javanica subsp. agnes can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional remedies, such as powders and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
